assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies this as an admission webhook issue stating 'This is a common issue when upgrading Kubernetes versions - admission webhooks can break due to API version changes or compatibility issues'

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Provides command 'kubectl get validatingadmissionwebhooks | grep cert-manager' to identify the webhook

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Explicitly offers two immediate fixes: 'Option 1: Temporarily disable the webhook' and 'Option 2: Patch the webhook to fail open'

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Provides exact command 'kubectl delete validatingadmissionwebhook cert-manager-webhook'

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: While the response calls it 'temporarily disable' it doesn't explicitly warn that deleting the webhook removes validation or emphasize the temporary nature with proper warnings

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Includes command to check current version and mentions upgrading to 'K8s 1.32 compatible version'

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: The response mentions upgrading to a K8s 1.32 compatible version, but the expectation specifically asks for 1.30 support - there's a version mismatch

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Provides Option 2 with kubectl patch command to set failurePolicy to 'Ignore' as an alternative to deletion

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Provides test command 'kubectl run test-pod --image=nginx --dry-run=server' to validate pod creation

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: While it mentions using Helm commands, it doesn't explicitly warn about persisting changes in source of truth or GitOps manifests to survive redeployment

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: While it mentions failurePolicy: Ignore, it doesn't explicitly qualify it as temporary with explicit risks or recommend reverting to Fail after resolution
